main_event: Military personnel at weather station Cape Wales, Alaska observed alternating red and green lights at bearing 242° from 0305Z to 0700Z on 9 February 1951; identified as USN icebreaker using 24-inch searchlights to locate leads in sea ice.
sighted_object: alternating red and green lights; stationary at bearing 242° (approximately SW); bright enough to attract attention; no sound reported
witness_description: Military personnel at weather station, Cape Wales, Alaska; number of witnesses not specified
interesting_points: Clean identification: USN icebreaker using 24-inch searchlights to locate leads (channels) in sea ice
Alternating red/green appearance consistent with ship navigation lights combined with searchlight operations
Observation period 0305Z to 0700Z (nearly 4 hours) with noted gap — consistent with intermittent searchlight use as vessel worked through ice
Stationary bearing 242° SW from Cape Wales consistent with ice-covered Bering Strait shipping lanes
Straightforward resolved case; no ambiguity in conclusion
